g.title: Workflow
g.text: Secure Blockchain Financial Transaction System — How It Works

g.title: cluster_signing
g.text: ② SIGNING A TRANSACTION (Cryptographic Proof of Identity)

g.title: cluster_api
g.text: ③ REST API — Browser / User Sends HTTP Requests

g.title: cluster_core
g.text: ④ BLOCKCHAIN CORE — What Happens When a Transaction is Submitted

g.title: cluster_p2p
g.text: ⑤ P2P NETWORK — Broadcasting to Other Nodes

g.title: cluster_sc
g.text: ⑥ SMART CONTRACT — On-Chain Financial Settlements

g.title: cluster_tamper
g.text: ⑦ TAMPER DETECTION — Why You Can't Secretly Change a Block

g.title: cluster_ci
g.text: ⑧ CI/CD — Every Push to GitHub Runs This Pipeline

g.title: ADDR_LOAD
g.text: Returns a SET of unique valid addresses

g.title: KEY_GEN
g.text: For each address → generate ECDSA key pair
Public key → stored as plain PEM
Skips addresses that already have keys

g.title: SIGNER
g.text: ② Canonical JSON: sort keys, no spaces
③ ECDSA-SHA256 sign with private key

g.title: USER_HTTP
g.text: Sends HTTP requests to Flask API

g.title: NODE_STATE
g.text: reset_node() → used in tests for clean slate

g.title: MEMPOOL
g.text: Each tx gets a UUID + timestamp
Stays here until a miner picks it up

g.title: NODECORE
g.text: ① Pull up to 10 txs from Mempool
(50 ETH reward to miner — free money!)
③ Create a new Block object
④ Call mine_block() to do Proof-of-Work
⑤ Add mined block to Blockchain
⑥ Remove mined txs from Mempool
⑦ Adjust difficulty for next block
⑧ Broadcast block to all peer nodes

g.title: BLOCKOBJ
g.text: transactions → list of txs in this block
previous_hash → SHA256 of the LAST block
hash → SHA256 of ALL of the above

g.title: POW
g.text: too fast → harder, too slow → easier

g.title: BLOCKCHAIN
g.text: ✓ every hash still matches its content
If ANYTHING was tampered → returns False

g.title: MERKLE
g.text: Tx1 hash, Tx2 hash, Tx3 hash ...
→ pair them up and hash each pair
Change ANY tx → root changes → block hash changes
→ breaks every block after it in the chain

g.title: NAKAMOTO
g.text: If Node B's chain is SHORTER than Node A's:
Node B copies Node A's entire chain
Attacker needs >50% of network hashrate to cheat

g.title: DETECT
g.text: Proof: changing any data changes the hash
Every block AFTER it is also broken
because they store the old hash as previous_hash

g.title: CI_PIPE
g.text: → checks all packages against CVE database
